key_insight: Any deterministic FEM-like multiscale space with fixed locality and fixed coefficient visibility cannot achieve uniform convergence on rough-coefficient elliptic problems; its worst-case Galerkin error stays bounded away from zero as H→0.
engineering_relevance: This sets a hard scalability limit for local multiscale surrogates in computational mechanics, showing that guaranteed accuracy on heterogeneous materials requires expanding the stencil, spectral dimension, or coefficient access.
method: Define structural scalability via bounded overlap and O(1) coarse-layer coefficient access
Prove a uniform lower bound using a finite family of periodic coefficients and smooth right-hand sides
Combine patch-matching, corrector approximation limits, positive-density mesh arguments, and periodic corrector convergence
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a rigorous worst-case L2-to-energy error lower bound that remains a positive constant independent of H, based on analytical proof rather than numerical experiments.
watch_item: The result is a worst-case impossibility theorem for deterministic local constructions, so practical methods may still work well on restricted coefficient classes or with nonlocal/adaptive enrichment.
paper_title: An Order-One Lower Bound on the Error of Scalable Generalized Multiscale Finite Element Space Constructions
